text.title: Scope-Creep Park List Template Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Scope-Creep Park List Template. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Off-scope ideas during a walkthrough are PARKED, not debated. Facilitator says one acknowledgement line and writes the row; debate happens at sprint planning.
rule.rationale: Mid-meeting debate destroys the decision-ask; parking keeps the meeting on track and respects the stakeholder.

rule.statement: Each parked row carries a named owner (typically the stakeholder who raised the idea) and the facilitator's handle.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership produces orphan ideas; the stakeholder must be re-engageable at revisit time.

rule.statement: Each row carries an explicit revisit_gate: 'next sprint planning', 'after launch', 'never (out of scope)'; vague 'later' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Vague gates produce backlog rot; explicit gates trigger calendar reminders.

rule.statement: Each row carries the verbatim acknowledgement line the facilitator used in the meeting (e.g. 'Good point — parking for sprint planning'); silent parking is rejected.
rule.rationale: Silently parked ideas feel dismissed; the acknowledgement line builds trust.

rule.statement: Park list is reviewed at the next sprint planning; rows graduate to (a) backlog row, (b) design-decision-log row, or (c) explicit 'closed — out of scope'. Rows untouched 2 sprints surface a warning.
rule.rationale: Without review, parked rows fester; the review keeps the bank honest.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do NOT run this methodology — emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition and route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above for flat methodologies), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-scope-creep-park-list-template.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
